idea.topic: what if you lived on a tidally locked planet
idea.hook: One side always faces the Sun?
facts: a tidally locked planet's sunny side can reach 3000°F, hotter than a football field of flames
the dark side can be -200°F, colder than 1000 miles of Antarctic winter
such a planet's habitable zone, if it exists, would be a thin ring around the middle, about the width of Texas
idea.payoff: and we still don't know if life can thrive in this eternal twilight zone
idea.title: Tidal Lock 🌎
idea.description: Imagine a world where one side always faces the Sun, and the other side is forever dark 🌑. This is the reality of a tidally locked planet, where extreme temperatures create a thin, habitable zone 🌡️. Can life survive in this eternal twilight zone? 🤔

scenes.text: One side always faces the Sun?
scenes.visual: A tidally locked planet slowly rotates, clearly showing a bright, sun-facing hemisphere and a dark, cold-facing hemisphere. A stark terminator line divides them.

scenes.text: SUNNY SIDE: 3000°F. Hotter than a football field of flames!
scenes.visual: Extreme close-up on the fiery, cracked surface of the sunny side. Heat shimmer effects and subtle, stylized flames lick across the surface. A distant, scorching sun glows.

scenes.text: DARK SIDE: -200°F. Colder than 1000 miles of Antarctic winter!
scenes.visual: Transition to an extreme close-up of the dark, icy hemisphere. Jagged, snow-covered mountains and frozen plains. Frost glitters, and a few icy meteors streak past in the background.

scenes.text: HABITABLE ZONE: A thin ring, width of Texas, in eternal twilight.
scenes.visual: A full view of the tidally locked planet. A glowing, thin green ring appears around the middle, highlighting the terminator line where the twilight zone exists. The planet slowly spins.

scenes.text: Can life thrive in this eternal twilight? We still don't know.
scenes.visual: Zoom into the green twilight ring. The light is dim, with a perpetual sunset/sunrise glow. Subtle, ambiguous silhouettes of speculative flora or fauna might be hinted at, then fade to a question mark.
